semantic.role: Defines the structural schema for a SpineFolderUnit, ensuring each folder unit in the ArchSpine mirror system adheres to a consistent format.
responsibilities: Specifying required fields (schemaVersion, directory, role, responsibility, children, provenance).
Defining the structure and types of child entries (files/subunits) within a folder unit.
Enforcing metadata provenance tracking (indexedAt, generatorVersion, pipelineStages).
invariants: All required fields must be present; no additional properties are allowed (additionalProperties: false).
The directory field must reference a valid scope path as per shared schema.
Each child entry must have filePath, role, and fileKind.
Provenance must include indexedAt (ISO timestamp), generatorVersion, and pipelineStages.

English.role: This schema defines the blueprint for folder units in ArchSpine. It ensures every folder unit (a directory in the mirror) is described uniformly, enabling automated processing and validation.

parameter_definitions.schemaVersion: Specifies the version of the schema used, ensuring compatibility across different releases of ArchSpine.
parameter_definitions.directory: The relative scope path within the repository that this folder unit describes. Must be a valid path per shared definitions.
parameter_definitions.role: A non-empty string describing the functional role of this folder unit within the system (e.g., 'source', 'configuration').
parameter_definitions.responsibility: A non-empty string summarizing the responsibility or purpose of the folder unit.
parameter_definitions.children: An array of child entries (files or subfolders) belonging to this unit. Each child must have a filePath (repo-relative), role, and fileKind. This enables recursive mirroring and validation.
parameter_definitions.provenance: Metadata about when and how this folder unit was generated. Includes indexedAt (timestamp of indexing), generatorVersion (version of the tool that created it), and pipelineStages (history of processing stages).

English.stability_and_risks: This schema defines invariants that, if violated, can cause failures in mirror synchronization, validation, and system integrity. The 'additionalProperties: false' constraint prevents unexpected fields from being injected, reducing security risks. The required provenance ensures traceability, which is critical for audit and debugging. Changes to this schema should be backward-compatible to avoid breaking existing folder units. The reliance on shared schema references means changes in shared.schema.json can propagate risks.
